title: cannot start rootless containers after starting and stopping them with systemd
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

After creating and starting a rootless container it happens very often that I cannot restart it. I have a systemd script to start it after boot.

I put SELinux in permissive mode for testing to make sure it is not an SELinux problem.

In my tests I can only reproduce this behaviour when I stop and start the containers with systemd. When I do the same thing directly with podman I don't have this issue (but once the container is in this failure state I cannot even start it directly with podman).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.
Write a systemd service for starting and stopping a rootless container. Place your service in ~/.config/systemd/user.
2.
Start and stop your container several times with systemd. Example
systemctl --user start podman-pgsql
systemctl --user stop podman-pgsql
3.

**Describe the results you received:**
After one or two cycles the container refuses to start.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Starting and stopping with systemd shoud work. If I define services with podman I want to have them available after booting my PC.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.7
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```


**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      runc version 1.0.0-rc8+dev
      commit: e3b4c1108f7d1bf0d09ab612ea09927d9b59b4e3
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 8589930496
  SwapTotal: 8589930496

  cpus: 8
  hostname: bat.localdomain
  kernel: 5.1.19-300.fc30.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 20m 53.39s
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com

  ConfigFile: /home/fansari/.config/containers/storage.conf

    number: 3
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /var/home/fansari/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 11
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
  VolumePath: /var/home/fansari/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
OS: Fedora 30 Silverblue

```

```
[Unit]
Description=PostGreSQL by podman

[Service]
Type=simple
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start -a pgsql
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop pgsql

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

We don't recommend using simple services for Podman - there are issues with that approach. Using a forking service with `--conmon-pidfile` on the container to make a PID file for systemd to use is preferred.

That being said, that `EOF` error is concerning. Can you try that again, and look for any logs from Conmon in journalctl? The real error should have been printed there.

I have now created a new container like this:

```
podman run -d --name=pgsql --conmon-pidfile=/run/user/${UID}/podman/podman-pgsql.pid --mount type=volume,source=pgsql,target=/var/lib/pgsql -p 5432:5432/tcp pgsql
```

I use this server:

```
[Unit]
Description=PostGreSQL by podman

[Service]
Type=forking
PIDFile=/run/user/%U/podman/podman-pgsql.pid
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start pgsql
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop pgsql

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

Now I can start and stop the container once with systemd. Starting it again fails.

```

Here some conmon messages:

```
Jul 28 18:55:51 bat.localdomain conmon[20950]: conmon d1b6a5499eddb9790983 <ninfo>: attach sock path: /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket/d1b6a5499eddb9790983c8b51e7e00b2954c7034b64012a9dc48e8be77044cf7/attach
Jul 28 18:55:51 bat.localdomain conmon[20950]: conmon d1b6a5499eddb9790983 <ninfo>: addr{sun_family=AF_UNIX, sun_path=/run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket/d1b6a5499eddb9790983c8b51e7e00b2954c7034b64012a9dc48e8be77044cf7/attach}
Jul 28 18:55:51 bat.localdomain conmon[20950]: conmon d1b6a5499eddb9790983 <ninfo>: ctl fifo path: /var/home/fansari/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/d1b6a5499eddb9790983c8b51e7e00b2954c7034b64012a9dc48e8be77044cf7/user>
Jul 28 18:55:51 bat.localdomain conmon[20950]: conmon d1b6a5499eddb9790983 <error>: Failed to mkfifo at /var/home/fansari/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/d1b6a5499eddb9790983c8b51e7e00b2954c7034b64012a9dc48e8be77044cf7
```
I'm going to guess that mkfifo is failing with EEXIST because the cleanup process is either failing or not running at all, but I'll look more into this tomorrow.

could it be a race between "podman stop" exiting before the cleanup process terminated?  At that point systemd is probably killing every other process left around, including the podman cleanup.

In that case it is necessary to use `KillMode=none`
Agree `KillMode=none` is probably a good idea

Great! This solved it. My containers survived several reboots doing it with this option.

So maybe RedHat should update their documentation.

https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_enterprise_linux_atomic_host/7/html/managing_containers/running_containers_as_systemd_services_with_podman

The example they give they use type=simple (not explicitly given, but simple is default) and they don't mention this KillMode option.

Maybe this issue does only affect rootless containers. I also have one root container where I never had problems with starting via systemd. But I have now also set this option there and it works.
We recommend using `podman generate systemd` to create services now, but I'll see who manages that page and ask about getting it changed.

In my example the result looks like this.

This means you have to recreate your service when you recreate your container because it contains the container id. Usually I don't care about the ID - I create a container for a specific purpose.

Also I want to mention that it does not work with this PIDFile definition in my case.

After starting the service the prompt is hanging for a while. I can see this error message:

```
Jul 30 18:45:22 bat.localdomain systemd[1125]: podman-pgsql.service: Can't open PID file /var/home/fansari/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/3bdfb12b2bff39228bac6729cb8bbb26f136d1a2918e176964ceb7f648908ea1/userdata/3bdfb12b2bff39228bac6729cb8bbb26f136d1a2918e176964ceb7f648908ea1.pid (yet?) after start: No such file or directory
```

When I replace it with "PIDFile=/run/user/%U/podman/podman-pgsql.pid" I don't have this issue.

```
[Unit]
Description=c232cd53f1311a2b4ff90a46f65fec7ad968b97b420af116243311ec9cb1150c Podman Container
[Service]
Restart=on-failure
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start c232cd53f1311a2b4ff90a46f65fec7ad968b97b420af116243311ec9cb1150c
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 c232cd53f1311a2b4ff90a46f65fec7ad968b97b420af116243311ec9cb1150c
KillMode=none
Type=forking
PIDFile=/var/home/fansari/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/c232cd53f1311a2b4ff90a46f65fec7ad968b97b420af116243311ec9cb1150c/userdata/c232cd53f1311a2b4ff90a46f65fec7ad968b97b420af116243311ec9cb1150c.pid
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```
Yeah, that `generate systemd` issue is known - will be fixed in Podman 1.5.0 (which hopefully releases on Friday).
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
Closing, as `podman generate systemd` will generate working unit files without this issue

Sorry for commenting on an old issue, but I'm using `generate_systemd` flag (see [Documentation](https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/collections/containers/podman/podman_pod_module.html)) of Ansible to create the unit files, and I face pretty much the same issue as described here. I assume it creates the unit files incorrectly under the hood, and I should run `podman generate systemd` directly instead? Maybe this should addressed directly in Ansible.

That definitely sounds correct. `podman generate systemd` changes progressively between releases and reflects the most current knowledge on how to best integrate Podman and systemd - trying to reimplement does not seem sustainable given this.
